fields.summary: Enable the batch mode of Optimal Reach payload.

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
Description

Enable batch mode processing for the Optimal Reach payload so that multiple segments sharing the same study date range are grouped and trained together in a single SageMaker job, rather than launching one job per segment. This dramatically reduces the number of training jobs needed (for example, 1000 segments can be processed in just 20 batch jobs of up to 50 segments each) while keeping single-segment requests working as before.

Acceptance Criteria

* Segments with the same study date range are automatically grouped into batches of up to 50 and processed together in a single SageMaker training job
* Single-segment requests continue to function unchanged through the existing non-batch path
* The total number of SageMaker jobs launched is reduced proportionally to batch size (e.g., 1000 segments results in approximately 20 jobs instead of 1000)

Definition of Done

This ticket is complete when the batch grouping logic is deployed in the full automation DAG and confirmed to correctly batch multi-advertiser training requests while maintaining backward compatibility for single-segment workflows.

Due Date

05/31/2026

fields.summary: [FE] Track GenAI summary section view metrics via Grafana Faro

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
**Update 26.05 – Changed requirements: Log emission conditions**

The implementation emits 3 log events (instead of the original 2). Conditions for each:

| Log Event | Fires when |
| --- | --- |
| `GenAiReportViewed` | User data is ready (`userId`, `reportId`, `countryCode` are available). Fires once per report view, regardless of summary presence. |
| `GenAiSummaryViewed` | All of: user data is ready + the summary section is at least 5% visible in the viewport (partial visibility). Fires once per report view. |
| `GenAiSummaryFullyViewed` | All of: user data is ready + the summary section is at least 90% visible in the viewport + summary content is fully loaded (`isSummaryReady`). Fires once per report view. |

  
  
**Summary**

Emit Grafana Faro log events when the GenAI summary section is displayed to the user. This data feeds the success metric: "% of Pre-Campaign Insights where summary viewed ≥80% (3 months)" defined in the GenAI Summary tech spec.

### Context

* The GenAI summary section is shown on the Pre-Campaign Insight report detail page when a generated summary exists (`GET /reports/{id}/genai/summary` returns content)
* The frontend already uses `@grafana/faro-web-sdk` (v1.2.3) with a reusable `useGrafanaLogs` hook (`src/hooks/use-grafana-logs.ts`) that wraps `faro.api.pushLog()`
* Existing usage pattern: `pushLog` with `LogLevel.LOG`, a `type` field in context, and relevant metadata (see `src/pages/campaigns/hooks/logs.ts` for reference)
* The summary section is gated behind a feature flag — metrics should only fire when the feature is enabled and the section is actually rendered

### Flow

1. User opens a Pre-Campaign Insight report that has status `COMPLETE_MEDIA`
2. Emit `GenAiReportViewed` event (denominator for adoption metric)
3. Frontend calls `GET /reports/{id}/genai/summary`
4. If summary content exists, the summary section renders
5. On section render (visible in viewport): emit `GenAiSummaryViewed` log event (numerator)
6. Grafana dashboard computes adoption rate: `GenAiSummaryViewed / GenAiReportViewed`

### Scope

* Create a dedicated hook (e.g., `useGenAiSummaryLogs`) following the pattern of `useCampaignActionGrafanaLogs`
* Emit `GenAiReportViewed` event with context: `{ type: "GenAiReportViewed", reportId, reportVersion, country, buyerId, userId }` — fires on every completed report view regardless of summary presence
* Emit `GenAiSummaryViewed` event with context: `{ type: "GenAiSummaryViewed", reportId, reportVersion, country, buyerId, userId }` — fires only when summary content is rendered
* Emit each event once per report view (deduplicate if component re-renders without user navigation)
* Only emit when feature flag is active
* Create a Grafana dashboard panel visualizing summary adoption rate (`GenAiSummaryViewed / GenAiReportViewed`)

### Out of Scope

* Chat interaction metrics (separate task)
* Feedback/rating tracking (separate task)
* Backend changes

### Dependencies

* GenAI summary GET endpoint (RSW-903)

### Definition of Done

- [ ] `GenAiReportViewed` event emitted exactly once when user opens a completed Pre-Campaign Insight report
- [ ] `GenAiSummaryViewed` event emitted exactly once when the summary section renders with content
- [ ] Events include `reportId`, `reportVersion`, `country`, `buyerId`, `userId` in context
- [ ] No events emitted when feature flag is disabled
- [ ] Grafana dashboard panel created showing summary adoption rate (target: ≥80%)
- [ ] Unit tests for the hook: emission logic and deduplication

fields.summary: Parameterize stress test fixture JSON with configurable inputs (advertiser, study period, region)

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
## Summary

Follow-up to RSW-966. Instead of static fixture files, create a more flexible approach to generating report request JSON files using parameterized inputs, so that stress tests can be run against varied configurations without manually editing JSON.

## Background

RSW-966 produces a set of static fixture JSON files under `tests/stress/reports/`. These cover a representative set of report types and sizes, but the inputs (advertiser/campaign, study period, region, audience size) are hardcoded.

This ticket was proposed in a comment on RSW-966 by Jonny Morrill (2026-05-11):

> "TODO: Create a follow-up ticket that will detail a more flexible approach to generating JSON files using parameterized inputs like advertiser/campaign and study period, region etc."

## Goal

Define a mechanism to generate fixture JSON files from parameterized inputs, enabling stress tests to be run with varied:

* Advertiser / campaign
* Study period (start_date / end_date)
* Region / country_code
* Audience size / type

## Acceptance Criteria

* A templating or generation mechanism (e.g. script, template files with variable substitution) that produces valid POST /reports payloads
* Parameters can be varied without manually editing JSON
* Generated fixtures are compatible with the stress test runner from RSW-135
* Documented in README

## Dependencies

* RSW-966 (static fixture corpus, must be completed first)
* RSW-138 (stress test tool merged to main)

status.description: This status is meant for an engineer be used to publish that they are working on the ticket
status.name: In Progress

fields.summary: Update the current contextual pipeline with an open-source audio information option

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
Description

Update the contextual analysis pipeline to include a free, open-source audio transcription option as an alternative to the paid AWS Transcribe service. This gives the team a cost-effective way to process audio locally without sacrificing functionality or accuracy, using the faster-whisper library for efficient performance.

Acceptance Criteria

* A local open-source audio transcription option (faster-whisper) is integrated and available as an alternative to AWS Transcribe, with both options togglable via configuration
* Native audio is enabled as the default setting for contextual analysis
* The transcription engine handles edge cases gracefully (e.g., skipping empty audio files) and provides transcript preview logging for debugging and QA validation

Definition of Done

This ticket is complete when the faster-whisper option is deployed, tested, and working correctly within the contextual pipeline, with configuration in place to switch between transcription providers as needed.

Due Date

05/28/2026

status.name: In Progress

fields.summary: Implement Specific Error Raising on Code Failure

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
## Requirements

* When code fails, raise specific/typed errors (not generic exceptions)
* Cover Snowpark errors, Snowflake DWH timeouts, and general report failures

## Acceptance Criteria

* Custom exception classes created for each failure category
* Pre-optimal reach raises specific errors on failure
* Post-optimal reach raises specific errors on failure
* Error messages include actionable context (query ID, stage, parameters)
* Unit tests cover each exception path
* Replicate the existing report cases
* Implement feature flag for snowpark OR reporting

status.name: In Progress

fields.summary: Pre-prod connectivity and auth configuration for stress testing

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
## Summary

Enable the stress test tool to connect to the pre-prod environment with proper authentication (SSO/service token) and document the setup.

This is also a spike research ticket to identify how we can reliably use a TOKEN for auth against pre-prod without a “refresh” token being expired when the user makes a new HTTP request from the browser. 

## Acceptance Criteria

* \[ \] Documentation for obtaining a valid pre-prod bearer token
* \[ \] Stress tool tested against pre-prod URL (e.g., `make run URL=<preprod-url> TOKEN=<token>`)
* \[ \] Graceful error messaging when token expires mid-run (checkpoint allows resume with new token)
* \[ \] Environment variable template or `.env.example` for pre-prod config
* \[ \] New TOKEN methods explored and potentially implemented

## Estimate

Medium — the tool already supports custom URLs; this is configuration + docs, but the auth method is still not viable long-term as it comes with issues and pain-points from a usage standpoint.

## Dependencies

* RSW-138 (merge stress tool into main)

fields.summary: [SRPOL-OR] Generate Example Audiences

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
As a regional lead, I want to be certain that the Optimal Solution can work fully and properly in my region. I will achieve this by running the Optimal Solution for my region and getting valid results.

At least 3 scenarios will be provided by regional leads to verify with minimum seed threshold.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

* Pipeline executes as designed
* Segments generated for ≥3 advertisers per viable market
* GBT model trained with documented configuration
* Model performance report delivered (AUC, precision, recall)
* Audience sizing methodology provided
* Data available and queryable in UDW

fields.summary: [BE] Auto-trigger summary generation in ai-processor-service

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
## Summary

Hook GenAI summary generation into the existing report processing pipeline. When a Pre-Campaign Insight report completes, automatically generate a summary so it's ready before the user opens the report.

## Context

* ai-processor-service runs scheduled jobs via gocron
* Reports transition to status `COMPLETE_MEDIA` when processing finishes
* The GenAI summary must be pre-generated at this point — zero latency for the user on report open
* If generation fails, the report lifecycle must remain unaffected

## Flow

1. Report processing completes → status transitions to `COMPLETE_MEDIA`
2. Check feature flag `genai-summary.enabled` for the report's context (country, buyerId, tenant)
3. If enabled: call `GenAIService.GenerateSummary(reportID, reportVersion, systemUserID)`
4. If disabled: skip silently
5. On error: log the failure, mark session as error — do NOT affect report status or processing pipeline

## Scope

* Identify the correct hook point in processor where `COMPLETE_MEDIA` status is set
* Add feature flag check with report context
* Invoke `GenerateSummary` (direct function call — no HTTP, no SQS)
* Graceful error handling: catch panics, log errors, never propagate failure to report pipeline
* System user ID for auto-generated sessions (no real user context in processor)

## Out of Scope

* Retry on failure (handled by <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-1037</custom> via user-triggered retry)
* Streaming / async notification to frontend

## Dependencies

* <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-878</custom> 
* <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-866</custom> 

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Hook identified and wired in ai-processor-service at COMPLETE_MEDIA transition
- [ ] Feature flag check with correct context (country, buyerId, tenant)
- [ ] GenerateSummary invoked as direct function call
- [ ] Failure does not affect report processing (error logged, session marked 'error', report status unchanged)
- [ ] Integration test verifying: trigger fires on report completion, skips when flag disabled
- [ ] make test passes, coverage ≥ 80%

status.description: This status is managed internally by Jira Software
status.name: Code Review

fields.summary: Update the current contextual similarity based output

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
Description

Enhance the contextual ad matching system to go beyond simple cosine similarity by supporting a composite scoring method that combines multiple matching signals. This allows for more nuanced and accurate ad-to-content matching while keeping the existing behavior as the default.

Acceptance Criteria

* The system supports a configurable scoring method per job that can blend semantic similarity, IAB category overlap, and content signal scores such as keywords, tags, objects, and brands
* A sentiment safety gate is in place that penalizes match scores when negative sentiment is detected on either the content or creative side
* Existing jobs continue to use the current cosine-only scoring by default, with no change in behavior unless the new method is explicitly enabled

Definition of Done

This ticket is complete when the composite scoring method is configurable per job, all new matching signals are integrated and testable, and the default behavior remains unchanged for existing jobs.

Due Date

05/21/2026

fields.summary: [Infra] Create request for Bedrock (IAM policy)

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
## Summary

Provision AWS Bedrock access (Claude Opus4.6) for the audience-insights service in pprod and prod environments.

## Background

The audience-insights service needs to invoke Claude Opus4.6 via AWS Bedrock. Bedrock access is managed centrally through the ep-bedrock repository, which provisions execution roles in the GenAI accounts. The kube2iam role in the consumer account then assumes that execution role to access Bedrock APIs.

Dev environment is already provisioned (<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-1024</custom> — done). This task replicates the same pattern for pprod and prod.

## Implementation Steps

1. `ep-bedrock` — Create Bedrock execution role (pprod + prod)

    * Module: bedrock-foundation v1.0.21
    * Model: us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1
    * Guardrails: medium preset
    * Trusted principals: pprod and prod kube2iam roles for audience-insights
    * Regions: us-east-1, eu-west-1
    
2. `audience-insights-infra` — Add sts:AssumeRole permission to kube2iam policies (pprod + prod)

    * Allows pprod/prod kube2iam roles to assume the Bedrock execution roles
    
3. `ep-terraform` — Whitelist execution roles in VPC endpoint policies (pprod + prod)

    * Add roles to bedrock and bedrock-runtime VPC endpoint allowlists
    

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] audience-insights pods in pprod can successfully invoke Claude Opus4.6 via Bedrock
- [ ] audience-insights pods in prod can successfully invoke Claude Opus 4.6 via Bedrock
- [ ] Access is scoped through guardrails (medium preset) and VPC endpoint policy

fields.summary: [Knowledgebase] Phase 4: Review, Polish and GA Sign-off

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
Quality assurance pass across all documentation. Ensure accuracy, consistency, discoverability, and formal sign-off before GA.

### Subtasks

* **Cross-team review of all documentation** — Eng reviews Support/Product docs for technical accuracy; Product reviews Eng docs for clarity; Support reviews User Guide for completeness
* **Test navigation and discoverability** — Have someone unfamiliar with the space try to find answers to 10 common questions; identify and fix gaps in navigation, labels, and cross-linking
* **Final review and sign-off for GA** — Product owner and tech lead sign off that the knowledgebase is GA-ready; verify no draft labels remain on critical pages

### Acceptance Criteria

* Cross-team review completed with all feedback addressed
* No pages with draft label in critical sections (Getting Started, User Guide, Support)
* Written sign-off from Product Owner and Tech Lead

### Dependencies

Blocked by Phase 2 and Phase 3 (all content must be in place before review)

fields.summary: Implement Specific Error Raising on Code Failure

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
## Requirements

* When code fails, raise specific/typed errors (not generic exceptions)
* Cover Snowpark errors, Snowflake DWH timeouts, and general report failures

## Acceptance Criteria

* Custom exception classes created for each failure category
* Pre-optimal reach raises specific errors on failure
* Post-optimal reach raises specific errors on failure
* Error messages include actionable context (query ID, stage, parameters)
* Unit tests cover each exception path
* Replicate the existing report cases
* Implement feature flag for snowpark OR reporting

status.name: In Progress

fields.summary: Pre-prod connectivity and auth configuration for stress testing

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
## Summary

Enable the stress test tool to connect to the pre-prod environment with proper authentication (SSO/service token) and document the setup.

This is also a spike research ticket to identify how we can reliably use a TOKEN for auth against pre-prod without a “refresh” token being expired when the user makes a new HTTP request from the browser. 

## Acceptance Criteria

* \[ \] Documentation for obtaining a valid pre-prod bearer token
* \[ \] Stress tool tested against pre-prod URL (e.g., `make run URL=<preprod-url> TOKEN=<token>`)
* \[ \] Graceful error messaging when token expires mid-run (checkpoint allows resume with new token)
* \[ \] Environment variable template or `.env.example` for pre-prod config
* \[ \] New TOKEN methods explored and potentially implemented

## Estimate

Medium — the tool already supports custom URLs; this is configuration + docs, but the auth method is still not viable long-term as it comes with issues and pain-points from a usage standpoint.

## Dependencies

* RSW-138 (merge stress tool into main)

fields.summary: [SRPOL-OR] Generate Example Audiences

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
As a regional lead, I want to be certain that the Optimal Solution can work fully and properly in my region. I will achieve this by running the Optimal Solution for my region and getting valid results.

At least 3 scenarios will be provided by regional leads to verify with minimum seed threshold.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

* Pipeline executes as designed
* Segments generated for ≥3 advertisers per viable market
* GBT model trained with documented configuration
* Model performance report delivered (AUC, precision, recall)
* Audience sizing methodology provided
* Data available and queryable in UDW

fields.summary: [BE] Auto-trigger summary generation in ai-processor-service

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
## Summary

Hook GenAI summary generation into the existing report processing pipeline. When a Pre-Campaign Insight report completes, automatically generate a summary so it's ready before the user opens the report.

## Context

* ai-processor-service runs scheduled jobs via gocron
* Reports transition to status `COMPLETE_MEDIA` when processing finishes
* The GenAI summary must be pre-generated at this point — zero latency for the user on report open
* If generation fails, the report lifecycle must remain unaffected

## Flow

1. Report processing completes → status transitions to `COMPLETE_MEDIA`
2. Check feature flag `genai-summary.enabled` for the report's context (country, buyerId, tenant)
3. If enabled: call `GenAIService.GenerateSummary(reportID, reportVersion, systemUserID)`
4. If disabled: skip silently
5. On error: log the failure, mark session as error — do NOT affect report status or processing pipeline

## Scope

* Identify the correct hook point in processor where `COMPLETE_MEDIA` status is set
* Add feature flag check with report context
* Invoke `GenerateSummary` (direct function call — no HTTP, no SQS)
* Graceful error handling: catch panics, log errors, never propagate failure to report pipeline
* System user ID for auto-generated sessions (no real user context in processor)

## Out of Scope

* Retry on failure (handled by <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-1037</custom> via user-triggered retry)
* Streaming / async notification to frontend

## Dependencies

* <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-878</custom> 
* <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-866</custom> 

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Hook identified and wired in ai-processor-service at COMPLETE_MEDIA transition
- [ ] Feature flag check with correct context (country, buyerId, tenant)
- [ ] GenerateSummary invoked as direct function call
- [ ] Failure does not affect report processing (error logged, session marked 'error', report status unchanged)
- [ ] Integration test verifying: trigger fires on report completion, skips when flag disabled
- [ ] make test passes, coverage ≥ 80%

fields.summary: Update the current contextual similarity based output

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
Description

Enhance the contextual ad matching system to go beyond simple cosine similarity by supporting a composite scoring method that combines multiple matching signals. This allows for more nuanced and accurate ad-to-content matching while keeping the existing behavior as the default.

Acceptance Criteria

* The system supports a configurable scoring method per job that can blend semantic similarity, IAB category overlap, and content signal scores such as keywords, tags, objects, and brands
* A sentiment safety gate is in place that penalizes match scores when negative sentiment is detected on either the content or creative side
* Existing jobs continue to use the current cosine-only scoring by default, with no change in behavior unless the new method is explicitly enabled

Definition of Done

This ticket is complete when the composite scoring method is configurable per job, all new matching signals are integrated and testable, and the default behavior remains unchanged for existing jobs.

Due Date

05/21/2026

fields.summary: [Infra] Create request for Bedrock (IAM policy)

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
## Summary

Provision AWS Bedrock access (Claude Opus4.6) for the audience-insights service in pprod and prod environments.

## Background

The audience-insights service needs to invoke Claude Opus4.6 via AWS Bedrock. Bedrock access is managed centrally through the ep-bedrock repository, which provisions execution roles in the GenAI accounts. The kube2iam role in the consumer account then assumes that execution role to access Bedrock APIs.

Dev environment is already provisioned (<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-1024</custom> — done). This task replicates the same pattern for pprod and prod.

## Implementation Steps

1. `ep-bedrock` — Create Bedrock execution role (pprod + prod)

    * Module: bedrock-foundation v1.0.21
    * Model: us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1
    * Guardrails: medium preset
    * Trusted principals: pprod and prod kube2iam roles for audience-insights
    * Regions: us-east-1, eu-west-1
    
2. `audience-insights-infra` — Add sts:AssumeRole permission to kube2iam policies (pprod + prod)

    * Allows pprod/prod kube2iam roles to assume the Bedrock execution roles
    
3. `ep-terraform` — Whitelist execution roles in VPC endpoint policies (pprod + prod)

    * Add roles to bedrock and bedrock-runtime VPC endpoint allowlists
    

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] audience-insights pods in pprod can successfully invoke Claude Opus4.6 via Bedrock
- [ ] audience-insights pods in prod can successfully invoke Claude Opus 4.6 via Bedrock
- [ ] Access is scoped through guardrails (medium preset) and VPC endpoint policy

fields.summary: [Knowledgebase] Phase 4: Review, Polish and GA Sign-off

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
Quality assurance pass across all documentation. Ensure accuracy, consistency, discoverability, and formal sign-off before GA.

### Subtasks

* **Cross-team review of all documentation** — Eng reviews Support/Product docs for technical accuracy; Product reviews Eng docs for clarity; Support reviews User Guide for completeness
* **Test navigation and discoverability** — Have someone unfamiliar with the space try to find answers to 10 common questions; identify and fix gaps in navigation, labels, and cross-linking
* **Final review and sign-off for GA** — Product owner and tech lead sign off that the knowledgebase is GA-ready; verify no draft labels remain on critical pages

### Acceptance Criteria

* Cross-team review completed with all feedback addressed
* No pages with draft label in critical sections (Getting Started, User Guide, Support)
* Written sign-off from Product Owner and Tech Lead

### Dependencies

Blocked by Phase 2 and Phase 3 (all content must be in place before review)
